artPrompt: An ornate hero shot of a grand bureaucratic cabinet of brass and dark wood, its many tiny council seats empty save for a single enormous coin spinning on a velvet pedestal at its heart, throwing flame-orange light and a sundial of long shadow spokes across the chamber. The coin foreshortened gleaming toward the viewer, embers and smoke drifting, sparks against night-black gloom, absurd ceremonial grandeur. Rendered as a bold painted graphic-novel splash — strong silhouettes, heavy directional inking and dramatic shadow shapes, fully painted (not flat cel comic).
name: The Coin-Flip Cabinet
text:
Every decision you would make this game, you instead flip a coin to make. Every one. Yes, including that one.
{R}, {T}: Flip three coins. For each heads, create a 1/1 red Goblin creature token. For each tails, the Cabinet apologizes and creates nothing. The Cabinet is very sorry.
If you ever make a decision without flipping, the Cabinet resigns in disgrace (sacrifice it).
flavorText: Five ministers. One coin. No accountability.
